title: anticharis glandulosa
intro: Anticharis glandulosa, also known as the fingered caltrop or fingered salsola, is a succulent plant species that belongs to the family Chenopodiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's extracts have been used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and combat bacterial infections, making it beneficial for overall skin health. Anticharis glandulosa can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or topical cream, and its extracts are also used in skincare products for their therapeutic benefits.
